topic.what: Vector-input collision-resistant PRFs constructed from compression functions
topic.how: Extend string-input keyed hashing modes KHC1/KHC2 by replacing compression function with string-input CR PRF to create VIM1/VIM2; introduces parallelizable PVIM
topic.why_matters: Enables efficient processing of structured/vector data as CR PRF; first dedicated vector-input CR PRF constructions enabling compact commitment schemes ccAEAD and post-quantum signatures

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of proposed vector-input keyed hashing modes
characteristics.evidence: Section 3 proposes VIM1 VIM2; Section 6 proposes PVIM
characteristics.context: Sequential VIM1 VIM2 from KHC1 KHC2; parallelizable PVIM requires more calls

characteristics.what_it_measures: Optimality of security bounds relative to cascade construction
characteristics.value: tight as Bellare et al cascade bound
characteristics.evidence: Corollaries 5-8 show bounds match Bellare et al
characteristics.context: PRF advantage bounded by underlying compression function RKA security

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of compression function evaluations for VIM1 VIM2 vs PVIM
characteristics.evidence: Section 6 last paragraph: PVIM needs more calls though parallelizable
Section 3.1 Alg 3 4 handle dim=0 case; needed for completeness
characteristics.context: Handles empty vector edge case unlike simple nesting

novelty: first dedicated vector-input CR PRF constructions
extended collision resistance notion for vector modes
formal security proofs for vector-input modes from compression function
parallelizable PVIM construction with collision-resistance proof
open_problems: constant selection criteria for optimal security
